description: Fix invisible text in the LifecycleSequence SVG illustration (one-time hook boxes have black text on near-black background due to opacity stacking over shadow rects), and document the new notifyReadinessChanged() SDK API across all relevant docs pages.
qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Fix invisible text in LifecycleSequence SVG illustration
userStories.description: The three one-time hook boxes (onActivate, onDeactivate, teardown) in the LifecycleSequence illustration have invisible text. The body rect uses fill='var(--color-primary)' opacity='0.12' which sits on top of a solid black shadow rect — the 12% opacity makes the background nearly black, and the black text is unreadable. Change the body fill to 'var(--color-background)' to match the repeating hook boxes.
acceptanceCriteria: onActivate box text (hook name + description) is readable against its background
onDeactivate box text is readable against its background
teardown box text is readable against its background
One-time boxes still visually differ from repeating boxes (solid vs dashed border is preserved)
'once' badges remain readable (white/primary text on dark background)
userStories.notes:
Files:
- docs/components/illustrations.tsx (lines 1383-1745, the LifecycleSequence component)

Context:
The LifecycleSequence SVG has 6 hook boxes on a vertical timeline. Three are one-time hooks (onActivate, onDeactivate, teardown) with solid borders and three are repeating hooks (onNavigate, onToolInvocationStart, onToolInvocationEnd) with dashed borders.

The bug is in the one-time hook boxes. Each has this structure:
1. Shadow rect: `fill="var(--color-foreground)"` (black) at offset (x+4, y+4)
2. Body rect: `fill="var(--color-primary)" opacity="0.12"` — nearly transparent yellow
3. Text: `fill="var(--color-foreground)"` (black)

SVG opacity="0.12" on the body rect means the black shadow shows through at 88%. Black text on near-black = invisible.

The repeating hook boxes work correctly because they use `fill="var(--color-background)"` (opaque white/dark) instead of `fill="var(--color-primary)" opacity="0.12"`.

Changes:
For all three one-time hook body rects, change `fill="var(--color-primary)" opacity="0.12"` to `fill="var(--color-background)"`. This makes them opaque like the repeating boxes. The visual distinction between one-time and repeating is preserved by the border style: one-time uses `strokeWidth="3"` solid border, repeating uses `strokeWidth="2" strokeDasharray="6 4"` dashed border.

Specific lines to change:

Line 1389-1398 (onActivate body rect):
```
Before: fill="var(--color-primary)" opacity="0.12" stroke="var(--color-foreground)" strokeWidth="3"
After:  fill="var(--color-background)" stroke="var(--color-foreground)" strokeWidth="3"
```

Line 1645-1654 (onDeactivate body rect):
```
Before: fill="var(--color-primary)" opacity="0.12" stroke="var(--color-foreground)" strokeWidth="3"
After:  fill="var(--color-background)" stroke="var(--color-foreground)" strokeWidth="3"
```

Line 1703-1712 (teardown body rect):
```
Before: fill="var(--color-primary)" opacity="0.12" stroke="var(--color-foreground)" strokeWidth="3"
After:  fill="var(--color-background)" stroke="var(--color-foreground)" strokeWidth="3"
```

That's it — remove `opacity="0.12"` and change `fill` from `var(--color-primary)` to `var(--color-background)` on exactly 3 rect elements.

Constraints:
- Do NOT change the repeating hook boxes (onNavigate, onToolInvocationStart, onToolInvocationEnd) — they already work correctly
- Do NOT change the 'once' badges — they use foreground fill with primary text, which is readable
- Do NOT change the shadow rects — they are correct
- Keep strokeWidth="3" on one-time boxes to preserve the visual distinction from dashed repeating boxes

userStories.title: Document notifyReadinessChanged() in SDK utilities page
userStories.description: Add notifyReadinessChanged() to the SDK utilities reference page. This is a new function exported from @opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk that lets plugins signal the extension to immediately re-probe isReady() instead of waiting for the 30-second poll.
acceptanceCriteria: notifyReadinessChanged is listed in the import block at the top of the utilities page
A new 'Readiness' section documents the function with signature, description, and usage example
The example shows a practical SPA login detection pattern
userStories.notes:
Files:
- docs/content/docs/sdk/utilities.mdx (add to import block + new section)

Context:
The utilities page groups SDK functions by category: DOM, Fetch, Storage, Page State, Timing, Logging. Add a new 'Readiness' category section.

Changes:

1. Add `notifyReadinessChanged` to the import block at the top (line 9-21). Add it after the `log` import on a new line:
```typescript
  notifyReadinessChanged,
```

2. Add a new section before or after the Logging section. Follow the existing section pattern:

```mdx
## Readiness

### `notifyReadinessChanged()`

Signals the Chrome extension to immediately re-probe `isReady()` for the current plugin. Call this when your plugin detects an auth state change (login or logout) in an SPA flow where the URL does not change.

Without this call, the extension relies on a 30-second polling interval to detect readiness changes. `notifyReadinessChanged()` triggers an immediate re-probe, making the state transition near-instant.

```typescript
import { notifyReadinessChanged, setAuthCache, clearAuthCache } from '@opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk';

// After detecting a login (e.g., JWT appeared in localStorage)
setAuthCache('my-plugin', token);
notifyReadinessChanged();

// After detecting a logout
clearAuthCache('my-plugin');
notifyReadinessChanged();
```

Safe to call in Node.js — no-ops silently outside the browser.
```

Constraints:
- Follow the existing section formatting pattern (## heading, ### function name with backticks, description, code example)
- Do NOT modify any existing sections
- The import block is the master import example at the top of the page — keep it sorted by category

userStories.title: Document notifyReadinessChanged() in plugin-class and plugin-development pages
userStories.description: Add references to notifyReadinessChanged() in the plugin class reference (isReady section) and the plugin development guide (implementing isReady section) so plugin authors know about the push-based readiness notification option.
acceptanceCriteria: The plugin-class.mdx isReady() section mentions notifyReadinessChanged() as a way to trigger immediate re-probing
The plugin-development.mdx 'Implementing isReady()' section includes an example showing notifyReadinessChanged() for SPA auth flows
userStories.notes:
Files:
- docs/content/docs/sdk/plugin-class.mdx (isReady section, around line 128-171)
- docs/content/docs/guides/plugin-development.mdx (Implementing isReady section, around line 107-145)

Changes to plugin-class.mdx:
After the existing isReady() example (line 170-171), add a brief note:

```mdx
By default, the extension re-probes `isReady()` every 30 seconds and on tab navigation events. If your plugin detects auth changes that happen without a URL change (e.g., SPA login flows), call [`notifyReadinessChanged()`](/docs/sdk/utilities#notifyreadinesschanged) to trigger an immediate re-probe.
```

Changes to plugin-development.mdx:
After the `waitUntil` async isReady example (line 144-145), add a new subsection:

```mdx
For SPA login flows where the URL doesn't change, call `notifyReadinessChanged()` to tell the extension to re-check immediately:

```typescript
import { notifyReadinessChanged, getLocalStorage } from '@opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk';

// In a tool handler or onNavigate hook, after detecting auth state change:
const token = getLocalStorage('auth_token');
if (token) {
  notifyReadinessChanged(); // Extension re-probes isReady() immediately
}
```

Without this call, the extension polls every 30 seconds — so a user logging in via a modal or XHR form might wait up to 30 seconds before the plugin becomes available.
```

Constraints:
- Keep the additions brief — these are cross-references, not the primary documentation (that's in utilities.mdx)
- Link to the utilities page section for the full API reference
- Do NOT modify the existing isReady() examples — add new content after them
